content.text:
<analysis>
a. Event type: Pull Request opened with merge conflict resolution across 7 gene review YAML files
b. This is a request for code review feedback - the user wants me to review the PR for code quality, potential bugs, performance, security, and test coverage
c. Key information:
   - PR resolves merge conflicts in 7 YAML files across different organisms (9CAUD, BPT4, DROME)
   - Files are gene review YAML files following a specific schema
   - All files passed validation according to the PR description
   - Changes show net reduction in lines (1868 additions, 2188 deletions)
   - Merge conflicts were resolved by blending upstream evidence/core-function blocks
d. Main tasks:
   - Read and analyze the modified YAML files
   - Check for YAML syntax and structure issues
   - Verify adherence to the gene review schema
   - Look for potential data integrity issues from merge conflict resolution
   - Check for consistency across files
e. Plan:
   - Update comment with todo list
   - Read the modified YAML files to understand the changes
   - Analyze the merge conflict resolution approach
   - Check for any formatting or structural issues
   - Provide comprehensive review feedback
f. No special permissions needed for this review task
</analysis>

input.body: ### PR Review in Progress Reviewing this merge conflict resolution PR for: - [ ] Analyze the 7 modified gene review YAML files - [ ] Check YAML structure and syntax integrity - [ ] Verify merge conflict resolution approach - [ ] Look for potential data integrity issues - [ ] Check consistency across files - [ ] Provide comprehensive review feedback [View job run](https://github.com/ai4curation/ai-gene-review/actions/runs/22749911340)

todos.content: Analyze the 7 modified gene review YAML files
Check YAML structure and syntax integrity
Look for potential data integrity issues

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

content.text: Let me read the modified YAML files to understand the changes and merge conflict resolution.

content.content:
1→id: Q9XJG2
     2→gene_symbol: darB
     3→taxon:
     4→  id: NCBITaxon:10663
     5→  label: Tequatrovirus
     6→description: DarB is a 251 kDa antirestriction protein from bacteriophage P1 that
     7→  protects phage DNA from host Type I restriction-modification systems, specifically
     8→  EcoB and EcoK. The protein contains an N-terminal Type II methyltransferase M.TaqI-like
     9→  domain and a central DExD-like helicase domain. DarB is incorporated into the phage
    10→  virion during assembly and is ejected into the host cell cytoplasm along with phage
    11→  DNA at infection initiation, where it provides protection against host restriction
    12→  systems. The protein is part of a multicomponent antirestriction system that also
    13→  includes DarA, DdrA, DdrB, Hdf, and Ulx proteins.
    14→existing_annotations:
    15→- term:
    16→    id: GO:0006304
    17→    label: DNA modification
    18→  evidence_type: IEA
    19→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000002
    20→  review:
    21→    summary: Too general - DNA modification occurs, but the biological function is
    22→      specifically antirestriction, not just general DNA modification
    23→    action: MODIFY
    24→    proposed_replacement_terms:
    25→    - id: GO:0099018
    26→      label: symbiont-mediated evasion of host restriction-modification system
    27→    supported_by:
    28→    - reference_id: PMID:3029954
    29→      supporting_text: The darA and darB gene products are found in the phage head
    30→        and protect any DNA packaged into a phage head, including transduced chromosomal
    31→        markers, from restriction
    32→- term:
    33→    id: GO:0008168
    34→    label: methyltransferase activity
    35→  evidence_type: IEA
    36→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
    37→  review:
    38→    summary: Too general - DarB has a methyltransferase domain, but specifically acts
    39→      on DNA
    40→    action: MODIFY
    41→    proposed_replacement_terms:
    42→    - id: GO:0009008
    43→      label: DNA-methyltransferase activity
    44→    supported_by:
    45→    - reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
    46→      supporting_text: UniProtKB keyword mapping and InterPro domain IPR011639 (Type
    47→        II methyltransferase M.TaqI-like domain) indicate DNA methyltransferase activity
    48→- term:
    49→    id: GO:0032259
    50→    label: methylation
    51→  evidence_type: IEA
    52→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
    53→  review:
    54→    summary: Too general and not the core biological process - methylation may occur
    55→      but the key function is antirestriction defense
    56→    action: KEEP_AS_NON_CORE
    57→    reason: While DarB has a methyltransferase domain suggesting methylation activity,
    58→      the primary biological function is antirestriction. Methylation is a molecular
    59→      mechanism, not the biological role
    60→- term:
    61→    id: GO:0005737
    62→    label: cytoplasm
    63→  evidence_type: NAS
    64→  review:
    65→    summary: Added to align core_functions with existing annotations.
    66→    action: NEW
    67→    reason: Core function term not present in existing_annotations.
    68→references:
    69→- id: GO_REF:0000002
    70→  title: Gene Ontology annotation through association of InterPro records with GO
    71→    terms.
    72→  findings: []
    73→- id: GO_REF:0000043
    74→  title: Gene Ontology annotation based on UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot keyword mapping
    75→  findings: []
    76→- id: PMID:3029954
    77→  title: Two DNA antirestriction systems of bacteriophage P1, darA, and darB - characterization
    78→    of darA- phages
    79→  findings: []
    80→- id: PMID:28509398
    81→  title: The multicomponent antirestriction system of phage P1 is linked to capsid
    82→    morphogenesis
    83→  findings: []
    84→core_functions:
    85→- molecular_function:
    86→    id: GO:0009008
    87→    label: DNA-methyltransferase activity
    88→  directly_involved_in:
    89→  - id: GO:0099018
    90→    label: symbiont-mediated evasion of host restriction-modification system
    91→  locations:
    92→  - id: GO:0005737
    93→    label: cytoplasm
    94→  description: Phage-encoded DNA methyltransferase that must be injected into host
    95→    cell to function
    96→  supported_by:
    97→  - reference_id: PMID:10515938
    98→    supporting_text: DNA methylase homolog DarB from bacteriophage P1 requires injection
    99→      into host
   100→suggested_questions:
   101→- question: What is the mechanism of DarB injection into host cells during phage infection?
   102→  experts:
   103→  - Phage biology researchers, especially those studying P1 phage
   104→- question: Does DarB protect phage DNA from host restriction systems or regulate
   105→    phage gene expression?
   106→  experts:
   107→  - Restriction-modification system experts
   108→- question: Are there host factors required for DarB methyltransferase activity?
   109→  experts:
   110→  - Host-pathogen interaction researchers
   111→tags:
   112→- lbnl-favorites
   113→status: DRAFT
   114→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→id: P04392
     2→gene_symbol: DAM
     3→product_type: PROTEIN
     4→status: DRAFT
     5→taxon:
     6→  id: NCBITaxon:10665
     7→  label: Enterobacteria phage T4
     8→description: 'T4 Dam is a DNA adenine methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.72) that catalyzes
     9→  the transfer of a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine (AdoMet) to the N6 position
    10→  of adenine within GATC palindromic sequences. The enzyme functions as a monomer
    11→  and methylates both cytosine- and 5-hydroxymethylcytosine-containing DNA substrates.
    12→  The primary biological role is to protect phage T4 genomic DNA from degradation
    13→  by the host E. coli restriction-modification defense system. This is a well-characterized
    14→  "orphan" methyltransferase with high-resolution crystal structures available (PDB:
    15→  1Q0S, 1Q0T, 1YF3).'
    16→existing_annotations:
    17→- term:
    18→    id: GO:0003676
    19→    label: nucleic acid binding
    20→  evidence_type: IEA
    21→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000002
    22→  review:
    23→    summary: T4 Dam binds DNA as part of its methyltransferase activity. Crystal structures
    24→      confirm DNA binding capability [PMID:12937411]. However, "nucleic acid binding"
    25→      is overly general for a protein with well-characterized DNA-specific binding
    26→      and enzymatic activity.
    27→    action: MODIFY
    28→    reason: This term is too general. The protein specifically binds DNA (not RNA)
    29→      in a sequence-specific manner (GATC recognition). More specific terms like GO:0003677
    30→      (DNA binding) or GO:0043565 (sequence-specific DNA binding) are more informative
    31→      and are already annotated.
    32→    proposed_replacement_terms:
    33→    - id: GO:0043565
    34→      label: sequence-specific DNA binding
    35→    supported_by:
    36→    - reference_id: PMID:12937411
    37→      supporting_text: 'T4Dam contains two domains: a seven-stranded catalytic domain
    38→        that harbors the binding site for AdoHcy and a DNA binding domain'
    39→    - reference_id: file:BPT4/DAM/DAM-deep-research-falcon.md
    40→      supporting_text: Crystal structures resolved a binary complex (Dam-AdoHcy) and
    41→        a ternary Dam-DNA-AdoHcy complex
    42→- term:
    43→    id: GO:0003677
    44→    label: DNA binding
    45→  evidence_type: IEA
    46→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
    47→  review:
    48→    summary: T4 Dam binds double-stranded DNA containing GATC sequences. This is well
    49→      established by crystal structures showing Dam-DNA complexes [PMID:12937411,
    50→      PMID:15882618] and biochemical studies demonstrating Km for DNA substrate of
    51→      1.1 x 10^-12 M [PMID:7782299].
    52→    action: ACCEPT
    53→    reason: DNA binding is an essential component of Dam function. The enzyme must
    54→      bind DNA to access and methylate target adenines within GATC sequences. While
    55→      sequence-specific DNA binding (GO:0043565) would be more precise, DNA binding
    56→      is accurate and represents core function.
    57→    supported_by:
    58→    - reference_id: PMID:7782299
    59→      supporting_text: the Km for substrate nonglucosylated, unmethylated T4 gt- dam
    60→        DNA is 1.1 x 10(-12) M
    61→    - reference_id: PMID:12937411
    62→      supporting_text: a DNA binding domain consisting of a five-helix bundle and
    63→        a beta-hairpin
    64→- term:
    65→    id: GO:0006260
    66→    label: DNA replication
    67→  evidence_type: IEA
    68→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
    69→  review:
    70→    summary: While T4 Dam methylates newly replicated phage DNA, the enzyme itself
    71→      is not directly involved in DNA replication (DNA synthesis). Dam acts on DNA
    72→      after or during replication to provide methylation-based protection, but does
    73→      not participate in the replication machinery itself.
    74→    action: REMOVE
    75→    reason: T4 Dam is not part of the DNA replication machinery. It is a DNA modification
    76→      enzyme that acts on replicated DNA. The methylation protects DNA from restriction
    77→      enzymes but does not contribute to DNA synthesis. This appears to be an over-annotation
    78→      based on the UniProtKB keyword "DNA replication" which may have been applied
    79→      due to the gene's role in the phage replication cycle rather than the enzymatic
    80→      mechanism of replication.
    81→    supported_by:
    82→    - reference_id: PMID:2510127
    83→      supporting_text: Bacteriophage T4 codes for a DNA-[N6-adenine] methyltransferase
    84→        (Dam)
    85→    - reference_id: file:BPT4/DAM/DAM-deep-research-falcon.md
    86→      supporting_text: T4 Dam is a DNA N6-adenine methyltransferase that transfers
    87→        a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine (AdoMet) to the N6 position of
    88→        adenine within GATC sites
    89→- term:
    90→    id: GO:0008168
    91→    label: methyltransferase activity
    92→  evidence_type: IEA
    93→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000120
    94→  review:
    95→    summary: T4 Dam is indeed a methyltransferase, specifically a DNA adenine methyltransferase
    96→      (EC 2.1.1.72). This is experimentally validated [PMID:2510127, PMID:7782299].
    97→    action: ACCEPT
    98→    reason: This is accurate but quite general. The more specific term GO:0009007
    99→      (site-specific DNA-methyltransferase adenine-specific activity) is also annotated
   100→      and is more precise. Keeping this general term is acceptable as it is not incorrect.
   101→    supported_by:
   102→    - reference_id: PMID:7782299
   103→      supporting_text: The bacteriophage T4 dam gene, encoding the Dam DNA [N6-adenine]methyltransferase
   104→        (MTase)
   105→    - reference_id: PMID:2510127
   106→      supporting_text: Bacteriophage T4 codes for a DNA-[N6-adenine] methyltransferase
   107→        (Dam)
   108→- term:
   109→    id: GO:0009007
   110→    label: site-specific DNA-methyltransferase (adenine-specific) activity
   111→  evidence_type: IEA
   112→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000120
   113→  review:
   114→    summary: T4 Dam specifically methylates the N6 position of adenine within GATC
   115→      sequences. This site-specific adenine methylation activity is the core molecular
   116→      function of the enzyme, experimentally demonstrated in multiple studies [PMID:2510127,
   117→      PMID:7782299].
   118→    action: ACCEPT
   119→    reason: This is the most precise and accurate molecular function term for T4 Dam.
   120→      The enzyme recognizes the GATC palindrome and methylates the adenine at N6 position.
   121→      This is the core enzymatic function and should be retained.
   122→    supported_by:
   123→    - reference_id: PMID:7782299
   124→      supporting_text: T4 Dam methylates the palindromic tetranucleotide, GATC, designated
   125→        the canonical sequence
   126→    - reference_id: PMID:2510127
   127→      supporting_text: Bacteriophage T4 codes for a DNA-[N6-adenine] methyltransferase
   128→        (Dam) which recognizes primarily the sequence GATC
   129→- term:
   130→    id: GO:0009307
   131→    label: DNA restriction-modification system
   132→  evidence_type: IEA
   133→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000002
   134→  review:
   135→    summary: T4 Dam is specifically an "orphan" methyltransferase - it is part of
   136→      a modification system but lacks a cognate restriction enzyme. The phage uses
   137→      Dam to protect its DNA FROM host restriction systems, not as part of a complete
   138→      R-M system of its own.
   139→    action: MODIFY
   140→    reason: This term implies Dam is part of a complete restriction-modification system
   141→      with both restriction and modification activities. T4 Dam is explicitly an orphan
   142→      methyltransferase (M.EcoT4Dam) that provides DNA protection without an associated
   143→      restriction enzyme. The appropriate term is GO:0099018 (symbiont-mediated evasion
   144→      of host restriction-modification system) which accurately describes the protective
   145→      role against host R-M systems.
   146→    proposed_replacement_terms:
   147→    - id: GO:0099018
   148→      label: symbiont-mediated evasion of host restriction-modification system
   149→    supported_by:
   150→    - reference_id: file:BPT4/DAM/DAM-deep-research-falcon.md
   151→      supporting_text: T4 Dam is explicitly an orphan methyltransferase (M.EcoT4Dam)
   152→        that provides DNA protection without an associated restriction enzyme
   153→- term:
   154→    id: GO:0016740
   155→    label: transferase activity
   156→  evidence_type: IEA
   157→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
   158→  review:
   159→    summary: T4 Dam catalyzes transfer of a methyl group from AdoMet to DNA adenine,
   160→      which is a transferase reaction. This is accurate but extremely general.
   161→    action: ACCEPT
   162→    reason: While accurate, this is a very high-level term. More specific terms (GO:0008168
   163→      methyltransferase activity, GO:0009007 site-specific DNA-methyltransferase activity)
   164→      are also present and more informative. However, transferase activity is not
   165→      incorrect and can be retained as a general parent term.
   166→    supported_by:
   167→    - reference_id: PMID:7782299
   168→      supporting_text: The bacteriophage T4 dam gene, encoding the Dam DNA [N6-adenine]methyltransferase
   169→        (MTase)
   170→- term:
   171→    id: GO:0032259
   172→    label: methylation
   173→  evidence_type: IEA
   174→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000120
   175→  review:
   176→    summary: T4 Dam catalyzes DNA adenine methylation. The enzyme is involved in the
   177→      biological process of DNA methylation to protect phage DNA.
   178→    action: ACCEPT
   179→    reason: This biological process term accurately describes a key function of T4
   180→      Dam - it carries out methylation of DNA. While this could be more specific (e.g.,
   181→      DNA methylation), it accurately reflects the enzyme's core biological role.
   182→    supported_by:
   183→    - reference_id: PMID:2510127
   184→      supporting_text: Bacteriophage T4 codes for a DNA-[N6-adenine] methyltransferase
   185→        (Dam)
   186→- term:
   187→    id: GO:0052031
   188→    label: symbiont-mediated perturbation of host defense response
   189→  evidence_type: IEA
   190→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
   191→  review:
   192→    summary: This term and its definition ("A process in which a symbiont interferes
   193→      with the ability of the host to mount a defense in response to its presence")
   194→      is designed for eukaryotic host defense responses. Bacteria (E. coli) do not
   195→      have "defense responses" in the GO sense - they have restriction-modification
   196→      systems, CRISPR-Cas systems, and other anti-phage mechanisms.
   197→    action: REMOVE
   198→    reason: This term is a SPKW-derived over-annotation and represents a semantic
   199→      mismatch. The GO term "host defense response" refers to eukaryotic immune and
   200→      defense mechanisms. Bacteria do not have "defense responses" as defined in GO
   201→      - they have specific anti-viral systems like restriction-modification and CRISPR-Cas.
   202→      The correct term for T4 Dam's role in protecting phage DNA from bacterial restriction
   203→      enzymes is GO:0099018 (symbiont-mediated evasion of host restriction-modification
   204→      system), which is already annotated and explicitly mentions phages and bacterial
   205→      restriction systems in its definition.
   206→    supported_by:
   207→    - reference_id: file:BPT4/DAM/DAM-deep-research-falcon.md
   208→      supporting_text: By methylating GATC, T4 Dam participates in phage counter-defense
   209→        against host restriction systems
   210→- term:
   211→    id: GO:0052170
   212→    label: symbiont-mediated suppression of host innate immune response
   213→  evidence_type: IEA
   214→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
   215→  review:
   216→    summary: This term is defined as "A process in which a symbiont inhibits or disrupts
   217→      the normal execution of the innate immune response of the host organism, the
   218→      host's first line of defense against infection." Bacteria DO NOT have an innate
   219→      immune response. This term is designed for pathogens/symbionts that infect eukaryotes
   220→      with immune systems, not for bacteriophages that infect bacteria.
   221→    action: REMOVE
   222→    reason: 'CRITICAL SEMANTIC ERROR: Bacteriophage T4 infects Escherichia coli, a
   223→      bacterium. Bacteria do not possess an "innate immune response" - that is a eukaryotic
   224→      immune system concept. The GO term GO:0045087 (innate immune response) is defined
   225→      as "Innate immune responses are defense responses mediated by germline encoded
   226→      components that directly recognize components of potential pathogens." This
   227→      is a eukaryotic concept involving cells like macrophages, NK cells, dendritic
   228→      cells, and pathways like NF-kB signaling. Bacteria have restriction-modification
   229→      systems, CRISPR-Cas systems, and abortive infection systems - NOT innate immune
   230→      responses. This annotation is a clear example of SPKW over-annotation where
   231→      the keyword "Inhibition of host innate immune response by virus" was incorrectly
   232→      applied to a bacteriophage. The correct term GO:0099018 (symbiont-mediated evasion
   233→      of host restriction-modification system) is already annotated and is accurate.'
   234→    supported_by:
   235→    - reference_id: file:BPT4/DAM/DAM-deep-research-falcon.md
   236→      supporting_text: T4 Dam acts in the infected E. coli cytoplasm on phage DNA,
   237→        methylating GATC sites as the genome is replicated
   238→- term:
   239→    id: GO:0099018
   240→    label: symbiont-mediated evasion of host restriction-modification system
   241→  evidence_type: IEA
   242→  original_reference_id: GO_REF:0000043
   243→  review:
   244→    summary: 'This is the correct term for T4 Dam''s biological role. The GO definition
   245→      explicitly states: "A process by which a symbiont evades the DNA restriction
   246→      modification system of its host. This process occurs in phages to protect themselves
   247→      from bacterial restriction enzyme systems. Some viruses encode their own methyltransferase
   248→      in order to protect their genome from host restriction enzymes." This precisely
   249→      describes T4 Dam.'
   250→    action: ACCEPT
   251→    reason: This is the most accurate biological process term for T4 Dam. The enzyme
   252→      methylates phage DNA to protect it from E. coli restriction enzymes. The term
   253→      definition explicitly references phages, bacterial restriction systems, and
   254→      virus-encoded methyltransferases. T4 Dam is a canonical example of this mechanism.
   255→    supported_by:
   256→    - reference_id: file:BPT4/DAM/DAM-deep-research-falcon.md
   257→      supporting_text: By methylating GATC, T4 Dam participates in phage counter-defense
   258→        against host restriction systems
   259→    - reference_id: PMID:12937411
   260→      supporting_text: DNA-adenine methylation at certain GATC sites plays a pivotal
   261→        role in bacterial and phage gene expression
   262→- term:
   263→    id: GO:0032259
   264→    label: methylation
   265→  evidence_type: IMP
   266→  original_reference_id: PMID:2510127
   267→  review:
   268→    summary: This IMP (Inferred from Mutant Phenotype) annotation is based on the
   269→      Miner et al. 1989 study that characterized dam mutants. The damh mutation (P126S)
   270→      produces a hypermethylating phenotype, demonstrating the enzyme's role in DNA
   271→      methylation in vivo.
   272→    action: ACCEPT
   273→    reason: This experimental annotation from PMID:2510127 provides direct evidence
   274→      that T4 Dam carries out methylation in vivo. The mutant phenotype studies showed
   275→      altered methylation patterns, confirming the enzyme's methylation activity.
   276→      This represents stronger evidence than the IEA annotation for the same term.
   277→    supported_by:
   278→    - reference_id: PMID:2510127
   279→      supporting_text: Hypermethylating mutants, damh, exhibit a relaxation in sequence
   280→        specificity, that is, they are readily able to methylate non-canonical sites
   281→- term:
   282→    id: GO:0009008
   283→    label: DNA-methyltransferase activity
   284→  evidence_type: IDA
   285→  original_reference_id: PMID:7782299
   286→  review:
   287→    summary: This IDA (Inferred from Direct Assay) annotation is based on the Kossykh
   288→      et al. 1995 study that overexpressed, purified, and biochemically characterized
   289→      T4 Dam. The enzyme was shown to have DNA methyltransferase activity with measured
   290→      kinetic parameters.
   291→    action: ACCEPT
   292→    reason: This is high-quality experimental evidence. The study purified T4 Dam
   293→      to near homogeneity and characterized its methyltransferase activity with precise
   294→      kinetic measurements (Km for AdoMet = 0.1 uM, Km for DNA substrate = 1.1 x 10^-12
   295→      M). This directly demonstrates the core molecular function of T4 Dam.
   296→    supported_by:
   297→    - reference_id: PMID:7782299
   298→      supporting_text: The Km for the methyl donor, S-adenosylmethionine, is 0.1 x
   299→        10(-6) M, and the Km for substrate nonglucosylated, unmethylated T4 gt- dam
   300→        DNA is 1.1 x 10(-12) M
   301→references:
   302→- id: GO_REF:0000002
   303→  title: Gene Ontology annotation through association of InterPro records with GO terms
   304→  findings:
   305→    - statement: Provides domain-based annotations linking InterPro domains to GO terms
   306→
   307→- id: GO_REF:0000043
   308→  title: Gene Ontology annotation based on UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot keyword mapping
   309→  findings:
   310→    - statement: Source of SPKW-derived annotations including the problematic immune-related terms
   311→    - statement: Keywords like "Inhibition of host innate immune response by virus" were inappropriately mapped to phage proteins
   312→
   313→- id: GO_REF:0000120
   314→  title: Combined Automated Annotation using Multiple IEA Methods
   315→  findings:
   316→    - statement: Combined automated annotation pipeline
   317→
   318→- id: PMID:2510127
   319→  title: Single amino acid changes that alter the DNA sequence specificity of the DNA-[N6-adenine]
   320→    methyltransferase (Dam) of bacteriophage T4.
   321→  findings:
   322→    - statement: Characterized damh hypermethylating mutant (P126S)
   323→      supporting_text: "Hypermethylating mutants, damh, exhibit a relaxation in sequence specificity"
   324→    - statement: Demonstrated T4 Dam recognizes GATC in both cytosine and hydroxymethylcytosine DNA
   325→      supporting_text: "Bacteriophage T4 codes for a DNA-[N6-adenine] methyltransferase (Dam) which recognizes primarily the sequence GATC in both cytosine- and hydroxymethylcytosine-containing DNA"
   326→    - statement: Identified homology region III as involved in DNA sequence recognition
   327→      supporting_text: "These results implicate homology region III as a domain involved in DNA sequence recognition"
   328→    - statement: damc mutant (F127V) methylates GATC in cytosine-DNA but not hmC-DNA
   329→      supporting_text: "damc, which methylates GATC in cytosine-containing DNA, but not in hydroxymethylcytosine-containing DNA"
   330→
   331→- id: PMID:7782299
   332→  title: Phage T4 DNA [N6-adenine]methyltransferase. Overexpression, purification, and
   333→    characterization.
   334→  findings:
   335→    - statement: Purified T4 Dam to near homogeneity
   336→      supporting_text: "was developed to purify the enzyme to near homogeneity"
   337→    - statement: Determined enzyme is a monomer (s20,w = 3.0 S, Stokes radius = 23 A)
   338→      supporting_text: "The MTase has an s20,w of 3.0 S and a Stokes radius of 23 A and exists in solution as a monomer"
   339→    - statement: Measured Km for AdoMet = 0.1 uM
   340→      supporting_text: "The Km for the methyl donor, S-adenosylmethionine, is 0.1 x 10(-6) M"
   341→    - statement: Measured Km for DNA substrate = 1.1 x 10^-12 M
   342→      supporting_text: "the Km for substrate nonglucosylated, unmethylated T4 gt- dam DNA is 1.1 x 10(-12) M"
   343→    - statement: Products (AdoHcy and methylated DNA) are competitive inhibitors
   344→      supporting_text: "The products of DNA methylation, S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine and methylated DNA, are competitive inhibitors of the reaction"
   345→    - statement: T4 Dam methylates canonical GATC; can methylate GAY at high enzyme:DNA ratios
   346→      supporting_text: "T4 Dam methylates the palindromic tetranucleotide, GATC, designated the canonical sequence. However, at high MTase:DNA ratios, T4 Dam can methylate some noncanonical sequences"
   347→
   348→- id: PMID:12654995
   349→  title: A nomenclature for restriction enzymes, DNA methyltransferases, homing endonucleases
   350→    and their genes.
   351→  findings:
   352→    - statement: Provides nomenclature system for restriction enzymes and methyltransferases
   353→      supporting_text: "A nomenclature is described for restriction endonucleases, DNA methyltransferases, homing endonucleases and related genes"
   354→
   355→- id: PMID:12937411
   356→  title: Structure of the bacteriophage T4 DNA adenine methyltransferase.
   357→  findings:
   358→    - statement: Solved crystal structure of T4 Dam in binary and ternary complexes
   359→      supporting_text: "We report here the crystal structures of the bacteriophage T4Dam DNA adenine methyltransferase (MTase) in a binary complex with the methyl-donor product S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine (AdoHcy) and in a ternary complex with a synthetic 12-bp DNA duplex and AdoHcy"
   360→    - statement: Confirmed two-domain structure with catalytic and DNA binding domains
   361→      supporting_text: "T4Dam contains two domains: a seven-stranded catalytic domain that harbors the binding site for AdoHcy and a DNA binding domain"
   362→    - statement: Two Dam monomers bind per DNA duplex in nonspecific mode
   363→      supporting_text: "the sequence-specific T4Dam bound to DNA in a nonspecific mode that contained two Dam monomers per synthetic duplex"
   364→
   365→- id: PMID:15882618
   366→  title: Transition from nonspecific to specific DNA interactions along the substrate-recognition
   367→    pathway of dam methyltransferase.
   368→  findings:
   369→    - statement: Characterized DNA recognition mechanism of Dam methyltransferase
   370→      supporting_text: "Three structures are described for bacteriophage T4 DNA-adenine methyltransferase (T4Dam) in ternary complexes with partially and fully specific DNA and a methyl-donor analog...We have identified two types of protein-DNA interactions: discriminatory contacts, which stabilize the transition state and accelerate methylation of the cognate site, and antidiscriminatory contacts"
   371→
   372→- id: file:BPT4/DAM/DAM-deep-research-falcon.md
   373→  title: Deep research summary for T4 Dam
   374→  findings:
   375→    - statement: T4 Dam is an orphan methyltransferase without cognate restriction enzyme
   376→      supporting_text: "T4 Dam is explicitly an orphan methyltransferase (M.EcoT4Dam) that provides DNA protection without an associated restriction enzyme"
   377→    - statement: Dam methylation protects phage DNA from host restriction systems
   378→      supporting_text: "By methylating GATC, T4 Dam participates in phage counter-defense against host restriction systems"
   379→
   380→core_functions:
   381→- molecular_function:
   382→    id: GO:0009007
   383→    label: site-specific DNA-methyltransferase (adenine-specific) activity
   384→  description: T4 Dam catalyzes the transfer of a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine
   385→    to the N6 position of adenine within GATC sequences. This is the core molecular
   386→    function of the enzyme, demonstrated by direct biochemical assays [PMID:7782299]
   387→    and structural studies [PMID:12937411]. The enzyme has high affinity for both
   388→    cofactor (Km = 0.1 uM for AdoMet) and substrate DNA (Km = 1.1 x 10^-12 M).
   389→  directly_involved_in:
   390→  - id: GO:0099018
   391→    label: symbiont-mediated evasion of host restriction-modification system
   392→proposed_new_terms: []
   393→suggested_questions:
   394→- question: What is the precise timing of Dam methylation during phage infection -
   395→    does it occur co-replicationally or post-replicationally?
   396→- question: Are there E. coli restriction systems that T4 specifically evades through
   397→    Dam methylation, and which host strains show differential susceptibility to dam-
   398→    phage?
   399→suggested_experiments:
   400→- description: Systematic analysis of T4 dam- phage viability across E. coli strains
   401→    with different restriction-modification systems to catalog the protective scope
   402→    of Dam methylation.
   403→  hypothesis: T4 Dam methylation provides selective protection against specific host
   404→    restriction systems, and dam- phage will show differential viability depending
   405→    on the host R-M system present.
   406→
